commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=31c77194beee481cc812360814f86d01c151c096)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds authoritative scan results for memory and session file enumeration, gates destructive pruning/reindex/export reconciliation on successful scans, and adds focused memory-core and memory-host SDK tests.

PR surface: Source +174, Tests +412. Total +586 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main swallows session and memory listing failures and then destructive full-enumeration pruning can consume the resulting empty listing. I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - A maintainer should resolve the public Plugin SDK API decision before automation updates baselines or hides exports.

target.title: fix(memory): do not prune session index from a failed directory scan
target.body:
## Summary

A full memory sync treats source enumerations as the source of truth for **destructive** maintenance: stale-row pruning of the session and memory-file indexes (`files` + `chunks` + vectors + FTS), full reindex rebuilds, and exported-session reconciliation. These enumerations swallowed filesystem errors and returned an empty listing, so a single transient scan failure (NFS `EIO`/`ESTALE`, permission blip) was indistinguishable from an empty directory and wiped the corresponding index — followed by an expensive full re-read/re-embed after the next successful scan. The failed-scan prune test in this PR fails on current `main` (the indexed row gets deleted), confirming the hazard is live.

### Sessions (original scope)

- `packages/memory-host-sdk`: add `scanSessionFilesForAgent(agentId): { ok, files }`. `ok: true` means the directory was actually read or authoritatively does not exist (see below); `ok: false` means the scan failed. `listSessionFilesForAgent` delegates to it and is unchanged for its non-destructive callers.
- `resolveMemorySessionSyncPlan` takes a required `scanOk` and returns `pruneStaleRows`, true only for a full enumeration that authoritatively read the directory.
- `syncSessionFiles` gates the stale-row prune on `pruneStaleRows`. A failed scan skips the prune (with a warning) and leaves the index intact for the next successful enumeration.

### Follow-up hardening (from review)

- A missing sessions dir is authoritative-empty only when the parent agent dir exists: fresh agents (the dir appears on first transcript write) prune orphans without log noise, while an unreachable state tree (e.g. unmounted volume) counts as a failed scan.
- `MemorySourceSyncPlan` carries `scanOk`; `runSync` no longer clears `dirty`/`sessionsDirty`/`sessionsDirtyFiles` after a failed scan, so the next eligible sync retries instead of reporting a clean sync that indexed nothing.
- The memory-file sibling gets the same guard: new `scanMemoryFiles(): { ok, files }` (exported via `engine-storage` and the plugin SDK barrel) with explicit root `readdir` probes — fs-safe `walkDirectory` swallows readdir errors even at the walk root, so an lstat-only check would still report `ok` on the exact failure class this PR targets. `syncMemoryFiles` skips its stale-row prune on a failed enumeration.
- Full reindexes abort (`assertReindexScanOk`) instead of swapping in an empty index built from a failed scan; the safe path restores the original DB on throw, the unsafe path skips `writeMeta` so the next sync retries the rebuild.
- `exportSessions` skips its reconcile-delete pass on a failed listing so one blip cannot delete the exported session-markdown corpus.
- Renamed the local CLI diagnostics in `cli.runtime.ts` (`scanMemoryFiles` → `diagnoseMemoryFiles`, `scanSessionFiles` → `diagnoseSessionFiles`) to avoid colliding with the new SDK export surfaced through the same barrel.
- Fixed a stale assertion in `manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts` that fails on `main` today: it still expects the pre-#89138 `void` return of `syncSessionFiles`.

### Why skip on *failed*, not on *empty*

Deletions/resets archive to usage-counted `.deleted.`/`.reset.` artifacts that still appear in the listing, and `disk-budget` can physically remove the last archive — so an **authoritatively empty** directory with a non-empty index is legitimately reachable. In that case we must still prune, or deleted-session content would linger in search forever. Skipping only on a *failed* scan prevents the wipe without introducing persistent orphan rows.

### Known residual gaps (follow-ups)

- Subtree readdir failures below the probed roots remain invisible (`@openclaw/fs-safe` `walkDirectory` reports no errors); blast radius is the affected subtree, not the whole index. A clean fix needs an additive fs-safe API (e.g. `WalkDirectoryResult.failedDirCount`).
- `resolveCanonicalRootMemoryFile` swallows workspace-root readdir errors; bounded to the single root memory file.

## Verification

Worktree rebased on `origin/main` (`48ec58a584`).

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src` — **860/860 passing**, including the new `manager-sync-ops.prune-guard.test.ts` (both sources: failed scan ⇒ no rows pruned and `scanOk: false`; authoritatively-empty / absent-dir ⇒ orphan rows pruned) and `manager-session-sync-state.test.ts` (`pruneStaleRows` matrix).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` on touched host-sdk files (`session-files.test.ts`, `internal.test.ts`) — passing (scan ok / authoritatively-empty / absent-dir-with-parent / unreachable-tree / failed readdir-after-lstat / failed extra path; `listSessionFilesForAgent` and `listMemoryFiles` unchanged on failure). Full `packages/memory-host-sdk` run has one unrelated under-load timeout flake in untouched `retry-utils.test.ts`; it passes in isolation.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:test:packages`, `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` — clean.
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts`, plugin-sdk boundary lints (`no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`), `sync-plugin-sdk-exports --check` — clean.
- `oxfmt --check` + oxlint on all changed files — clean.
- Hazard proof on `main`: running this PR's failed-scan prune test against clean `origin/main` deletes the indexed session row (`expected ['sessions/main/thread.jsonl'] to deeply equal []`).

### Known gaps (CI/Testbox)

- Full `pnpm build` not run locally in the worktree — recommend CI coverage.
- `plugin-sdk:surface:check` / `plugin-sdk:api:check` not re-run locally after the final rebase (both were failing on pre-rebase `main` against stale baselines; `main` has since regenerated them). If CI flags the two intentional new exports (`scanSessionFilesForAgent`, `scanMemoryFiles`), a `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` baseline bump can ride along.

## Notes

- #91081 (session-listing cache perf PR, still open) is complementary: it reduces READDIR load; this PR makes the destructive consumers stop trusting failed listings. No conflicts beyond both touching `session-files.ts` (additive here).
target.author: amknight

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
